_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

Discovery is refined. `docs/PRD.md` has not been created and no implementation code has been written — both were off-limits per the request. Here is the close-out summary you asked for.

---

### 1. What changed in discovery

`docs/discovery/NOTES.md`:

- **Header.** `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` re-labelled **background only** (not "background / template" — no scope contribution). New gate criterion: scope tension is **resolved in this document**, not deferred to PRD. New gate criterion: submission artefacts are first-class. Status updated to "Draft (refined — corrective pass)".
- **§1 Problem.** Removed "confirm an arrangement they can actually keep" framing and the "PRD will decide how far the product reaches beyond reflection" hedge. Added a positive scope statement: this is a customer-facing reflection + tracking surface; the brief does not ask the product to book arrangements, confirm plans, expose an arrangements endpoint, or feed an agent queue.
- **§2 Users.** Deleted the "Secondary — financial support agent (COps)" subsection. Reviewer is now "Secondary". Added an explicit **"Out-of-scope user roles"** subsection naming COps / financial-support agents and creditors / portfolio managers.
- **§3 Why now.** Rewrote the "agent channels" line to say "human support channels" and clarified the relief comes from the customer-facing reflection, not from routing work to agents.
- **§4 Success signals.** Added a new **Submission artefacts** subsection citing brief lines 76–93 and 104–113 (README, prompt history, `DECISIONS.md`, time-spent). Removed "DECISIONS.md" mention from Reviewer outcome and gave it its own first-class home. Edited Quantitative metrics to call out that "time-to-confirmation" / "customers reaching a confirmed plan" targets are **not** appropriate — no confirmation surface exists.
- **§5 Constraints.** Workflow section expanded into a labelled mandatory-deliverables list (`README`, prompt history, `DECISIONS.md`, time-spent). Added "Don't over-engineer the submission" as a constraint on PRD requirement count / tech-spec abstraction count. CONC row rewritten to remove the "record what was offered/accepted" arrangement-journey implication.
- **§6 OQ-1.** Upgraded from "DIRECTION SET (default)" to **RESOLVED (corrective pass)**. Status table row updated. PRD is told not to re-open it.
- **§7 Out of scope.** Restructured into three labelled blocks: (a) artefact-level, (b) **product-scope dropped from S001** (new — enumerates the items you listed), (c) ongoing S001-deferred items.
- **Traceability table.** Replaced the OQ-1 "meta" row with a closed-at-discovery marker. Rewrote the "handoff to human support" row as a signposting requirement (explicitly **not** an agent queue / callback / handover). Added a new row for submission artefacts.

Bookkeeping per the `ai-history` rule:

- `docs/ai/sessions/S003-discovery.md` updated with a corrective-refinement prompt entry and decision **D-13** that enumerates the dropped items and the artefact promotion.
- `docs/PROMPT_HISTORY.md` S003 row updated to reflect the corrective pass.

### 2. What is now in scope

Anchored to the brief (and only the brief):

- **Meaningful affordability assessment** displayed to the customer — disposable income + categorical band (surplus / breakeven / shortfall) + plain-language explainer + delta vs the previous snapshot (brief MUST 1; OQ-3).
- **Financial position tracking over time** via per-submission immutable snapshots; submission *is* the snapshot event (brief MUST 2; OQ-2).
- **Well-targeted tests** on the cases that actually matter, not happy paths (brief MUST 3).
- **Edge cases as first-class outcomes**: income = 0, expenditure > income, no-data state, invalid input — all surfaced with clear messages, not form errors (brief SHOULD; OQ-4).
- **Vulnerability-aware presentation**: empathetic copy, optional self-declaration, escape hatch to human support (signposting only), accessibility baseline (brief SHOULD; §4 / §6).
- **FCA-shaped data handling and explainability**: data minimisation, no PII in logs, no synthetic-but-unsafe fixtures, explainable affordability surface (brief lines 17–19, 69–70).
- **Persistence of snapshot history** via SQLite + lightweight ORM (PRD-level requirement; ORM/schema/file location is tech-spec's call; OQ-5).
- **Seven fixture personas** (surplus / breakeven / shortfall / zero-income / new-customer / irregular-income / joint-income) as repo fixtures, not DB state (OQ-6).
- **Submission artefacts as deliverables**: `README` runnable on a fresh clone, full prompt history retained, `DECISIONS.md` at repo root (built / left-out / next-up / why), time-spent note (brief lines 76–93, 104–113).

Stretch items are recorded but not committed: currency + country_code with migrations, time-limited statement-share links, branded PDF export.

### 3. What is explicitly out of scope

Per the corrective pass, the following items are **dropped** and PRD must not reintroduce them (`docs/discovery/NOTES.md` §7(b)):

- Repayment-arrangement booking
- Confirmation journey (I&E → options → confirm wizard)
- Payment-plan confirmation
- `POST /api/arrangements` (or any equivalent arrangements endpoint)
- Agent review queues
- Agent-facing console or override UI
- COps-readable submission objects beyond what the customer surface naturally produces

Carried forward from S001's deferrals (§7(c)): real authentication, Open Banking, credit checks, payments processing, CRM integration, email/SMS notifications, full vulnerability-assessment workflow, Welsh translation, exhaustive E2E suite.

Artefact-level out of scope for this phase (§7(a)): PRD requirement IDs, acceptance criteria, numeric targets, tech-spec decisions, test plans, fixtures, implementation code, independent FCA citation verification.

### 4. Whether `/prd` can be run next

**Yes.** Gate criteria are met:

- Problem, users, constraints, success signals, and open questions are populated.
- Every concrete claim cites a repo source or is marked `TBC`.
- Traceability table maps each discovery insight to a draft `R?` slot or a "closed at discovery" marker.
- Scope tension is resolved at discovery (OQ-1 RESOLVED), not deferred — PRD inherits a clean scope boundary.
- Submission artefacts are recorded as first-class success signals (§4) and constraints (§5).
- All other open questions (OQ-2 through OQ-7) are direction-set, leaving PRD only thresholds / copy / acceptance criteria to author.

When you invoke `/prd`, the canonical structure in `docs/PRD_TEMPLATE.md` already aligns with this stance (`docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` listed as background only). PRD must turn OQ-2 through OQ-7 into `R*` requirements with acceptance criteria, must add the submission-artefact requirement, and must not reintroduce any §7(b) item.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

All tasks complete. The brief is now the **sole** binding source for scope; the redundant pre-brief analysis is gone; every remaining mention of `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` in tracked docs is appropriate (historical record, supersession marker, or PRD-must-not-cite warning). Here is the close-out you requested.

---

### 1. Inconsistencies found

When auditing `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` against the brief and `docs/discovery/NOTES.md`, I found that `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` was **fundamentally about the dropped scope**, not a parallel framing of the brief:

- **MUSTs (M1–M10) were collections-workflow MUSTs.** M1 (arrears summary), M4 (repayment options), M5 (plan selection & confirmation), M6 (`POST /api/arrangements` + confirmation state), and M7 (validation including "plan unaffordable vs stated disposable income") are the exact items D-13 already dropped — but they were sitting in `TASK_ANALYSIS.md` MUSTs as if normative.
- **SHOULDs (S1, S5) framed the product as a wizard.** S1 ("Multi-step journey: overview → I&E → options → confirm") and S5 ("Server authority: plan generation re-validated on submit") only make sense if you are building the dropped collections journey.
- **Stretch (X1, X2, X4) included agent-facing scope.** X2 was an explicit "Agent view — read-only internal screen listing submitted arrangements"; X4 was "Audit trail of plan offered → selected → confirmed". Both are in §7(b) dropped scope.
- **§3 Implied requirements mandated a "COps-ready payload."** Direct conflict with the agent-review-process exclusion.
- **§6 Option A (the "recommended baseline") was the collections journey.** "Multi-step App Router UI; affordability/plan engine... Route Handlers accept/validate submissions into JSON or in-memory store." Built around the journey, not the reflection surface.
- **§7 Recommended MVP §1–§7 was the collections workflow.** Step list literally read "Three-step journey: I&E → affordable options → review & confirm" and included "POST `/api/arrangements`" and "Confirmation page".
- **Cross-doc citation pollution.** `docs/discovery/NOTES.md` carried 28+ citations to `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md`, each one a small load-bearing reference (FCA paraphrases, vulnerability framing, mock-auth assumption, timebox, etc.). Keeping the doc as "background only" meant the discovery notes were structurally dependent on a document whose scope contradicted the brief.
- **Stale forward-looking references.** `docs/PRD_TEMPLATE.md`, `.rulesync/commands/discovery.md`, `.rulesync/commands/prd.md`, `docs/ai/sessions/S002-ai-guardrails.md` all still listed `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` as an input to load or as background.

**Decision rationale (per your explicit permission):** "Refining" `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` would have meant rewriting it as a duplicate of `docs/discovery/NOTES.md` with most of its sections deleted or inverted. Withdrawal is the cleaner, more honest move.

### 2. Corrections made

**Deleted**

- `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` — removed from the repo entirely.

**Substance preserved by inlining into `docs/discovery/NOTES.md`** (now owned by the discovery doc, marked "_not independently verified_ against the FCA source" where appropriate):

- FCA Consumer Duty / CONC / FG21/1 paraphrases (§2 Vulnerable customers; §5 Regulatory table).
- UK GDPR paraphrase (§5 Data protection table).
- User-empathy bullets — anxious/time-poor/ashamed, irregular income, underestimating essentials, zero/negative disposable income (§2 Primary).
- Why-now paraphrases — Consumer Duty fair-value concern; operational cost on support channels (§3).
- Working timebox derivation — 3–5 focused days (§5 Timebox).
- Mock-auth assumption (A-2).
- Stretch-deferrals rationale (A-3).
- "No dark patterns" principle (§4 Customer outcome).

**Scope corrections in `docs/discovery/NOTES.md` §7(b)** — expanded the dropped list with the items you named today:

- Repayment-plan selection
- Arrangement confirmation
- Collections workflow
- Agent review processes
- Arrears-management tooling

These join the D-13 dropped items already present (arrangement booking, payment-plan confirmation, `POST /api/arrangements`, agent console / override UI, COps-specific payloads). 28+ `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` citations removed; remaining mentions are exclusively historical / explanatory (header note about the withdrawal, OQ-1 resolution narrative, §7(b) provenance, traceability row).

**Consequential cleanups in the rest of the repo**

- `.rulesync/commands/discovery.md` — removed `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` from "Inputs to load"; added a withdrawal note.
- `.rulesync/commands/prd.md` — removed `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` from "Inputs to load"; added a must-not-cite-or-reintroduce note listing the dropped items.
- `docs/PRD_TEMPLATE.md` — `Inputs consumed` no longer lists the withdrawn doc; new "Withdrawn input" line records the supersession and points PRD to `docs/discovery/NOTES.md` §7(b).
- `docs/ai/sessions/S001-task-analysis.md` — added "Status update (added by S003 D-14)" block; original handoff items struck through as superseded. The snapshot itself is preserved for prompt-history transparency.
- `docs/ai/sessions/S002-ai-guardrails.md` — added in-place supersession markers to the two historical references (one in "Context given to AI", one in "Handoff for next session") rather than rewriting historical content.
- `docs/ai/sessions/S003-discovery.md` — added D-14 (consistency-and-withdrawal pass) with full change list; updated "Files changed" and "Handoff for next session" accordingly.
- `docs/PROMPT_HISTORY.md` — S001 row marked "Artefact withdrawn in S003 D-14"; S003 row updated to record the withdrawal as a session outcome and list the now-final §7(b) dropped scope.

**Generated tooling note:** `.rulesync/commands/` is the source; `.cursor/commands/` and `.claude/commands/` are generated (gitignored). The `/discovery` and `/prd` commands you have loaded right now still reference the withdrawn doc; running `npm run rulesync -- generate` will regenerate them from the updated `.rulesync/` sources.

### 3. Confirmation that discovery is PRD-ready

**Yes — discovery is PRD-ready.** Gate criteria check:

- **Problem, users, constraints, success signals, open questions populated** — ✓ (`docs/discovery/NOTES.md` §1–§6).
- **Every concrete claim cites a non-withdrawn source, is a labelled paraphrase, or is `TBC`** — ✓. All `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` citations either removed or repurposed as explanatory historical markers. FCA / GDPR paraphrases now owned by the discovery doc.
- **Traceability table maps each discovery insight to a draft `R?` slot or "closed at discovery"** — ✓.
- **Scope is closed at discovery, not deferred to PRD** — ✓. OQ-1 RESOLVED (D-13 + D-14). §7(b) enumerates all dropped items including the five you named today.
- **Submission artefacts as first-class success signals + constraints** — ✓ (§4 Submission artefacts; §5 Workflow).
- **OQ-2 through OQ-7 direction-set, ready for PRD to ratify as `R*` requirements with acceptance criteria** — ✓.
- **No parallel scope document exists** — ✓. `docs/TASK_ANALYSIS.md` deleted; PRD only needs the brief + `docs/discovery/NOTES.md`.

When you invoke `/prd`, the canonical inputs are `docs/Ophelos Engineering Take-Home Task.pdf` and `docs/discovery/NOTES.md` only. The PRD template already aligns. The dropped-scope list in `docs/discovery/NOTES.md` §7(b) is the boundary the PRD must not cross.
